sections.title: DEFINITION OF PRODUCE
sections.text: This Standard applies to commercial varieties of guavas grown from Psidium guajava L., of the Myrtaceae family, to be supplied fresh to the consumer, after preparation and packaging. Guavas for industrial processing are excluded.
sections.retrieval_text:
DEFINITION OF PRODUCE
number=1
level=1
This Standard applies to commercial varieties of guavas grown from Psidium guajava L., of the Myrtaceae family, to be supplied fresh to the consumer, after preparation and packaging. Guavas for industrial processing are excluded.

page_spans.text: This Standard applies to commercial varieties of guavas grown from Psidium guajava L., of the Myrtaceae family, to be supplied fresh to the consumer, after preparation and packaging. Guavas for industrial processing are excluded.

sections.title: The guavas must have reached an appropriate degree of development and ripeness in accordance with criteria proper to the variety and to the area in which they are grown.
path_titles: The guavas must have reached an appropriate degree of development and ripeness in accordance with criteria proper to the variety and to the area in which they are grown.
sections.text:
The development and condition of the guavas must be such as to enable them: 
­ to withstand transport and handling; and 
­ to arrive in satisfactory condition at the place of destination.

sections.title: “Extra” Class
sections.text: Guavas in this class must be of superior quality. They must be characteristic of the variety and/or commercial type. They must be free of defects, with the exception of very slight superficial defects, provided these do not affect the general appearance of the produce, the quality, the keeping quality and presentation in the package.
sections.retrieval_text:
PROVISIONS CONCERNING QUALITY > Classification > “Extra” Class
number=2.2.1
level=3
Guavas in this class must be of superior quality. They must be characteristic of the variety and/or commercial type. They must be free of defects, with the exception of very slight superficial defects, provided these do not affect the general appearance of the produce, the quality, the keeping quality and presentation in the package.

page_spans.text: Guavas in this class must be of superior quality. They must be characteristic of the variety and/or commercial type. They must be free of defects, with the exception of very slight superficial defects, provided these do not affect the general appearance of the produce, the quality, the keeping quality and presentation in the package.

sections.title: Class I
sections.retrieval_text:
PROVISIONS CONCERNING QUALITY > Classification > Class I
number=2.2.2
level=3
Guavas in this class must be of good quality. They must be characteristic of the variety and/or commercial type. The following slight defects, however, ma y be allowed, provided these do not affect the general appearance of the produce, the quality, the keeping quality and presentation in the package:  
­ slight defects of colour or shape; 
­ slight defects on the skin due to rubbing and other superficial defects such as sunburns, blemishes and scabs not exceeding 5% of the total surface area. The defects must not, in any case, affect the pulp of the fruit.

sections.title: Class II
sections.retrieval_text:
PROVISIONS CONCERNING QUALITY > Classification > Class II
number=2.2.3
level=3
This class includes guavas which do not qualify for inclusion in the higher classes, but satisfy the minimum requirements specified in Section 2.1 above. The following defects, however, may be allowed, provided the guavas retain their essential characteristics as regards the quality, the keeping quality and presentation:  
­ defects in shape and colour; 
­ defects on the skin due to rubbing and  other defects such as sunburns, blemishes and scabs  not exceeding 10% of the total surface area. The defects must not, in any case, affect the pulp of the fruit.

sections.title: PROVISIONS CONCERNING SIZING
sections.text:
Size is determined by the weight or maximum diameter of the equatorial section of the fruit, in accordance with the following table:
Size Code Weight (g) Diameter (mm)
1 > 450 > 100
2 351 - 450 96 - 100
3 251 - 350 86 - 95
4 201 - 250 76 - 85
5 151 - 200 66 - 75
6 101 - 150 54 - 65
7 61 - 100 43 - 53
8 35 - 60 30 - 42
9 < 35 < 30

sections.title: PROVISIONS CONCERNING TOLERANCES
sections.text: Tolerances in respect of quality and size shall be allowed in each package for produce not satisfying the requirements of the class indicated.

sections.title: Size Tolerances
sections.text: For all classes, 10% by number or weight of guavas corresponding to the size immediately above or below that indicated on the package.

sections.title: Uniformity
sections.text: The contents of each package must be uniform and contain only guavas of the same origin, variety and/or commercial type, quality and size. The visible part of the contents of the package must be representative of the entire contents.

page_spans.text: The contents of each package must be uniform and contain only guavas of the same origin, variety and/or commercial type, quality and size. The visible part of the contents of the package must be representative of the entire contents.

sections.title: Packaging
sections.text:
Guavas must be packed in such a way as to protect the produce properly. The materials used inside the package must be new1, clean, and of a quality such as to avoid causing any external or internal damage to the produce. The use of materials, particularly of paper or stamps bearing trade specifications is allowed, provided the printing or labelling has been done with non-toxic ink or glue.
Guavas shall be packed in each container in compliance with the Code of Practice for Packaging and Transport of Fresh Fruits and Vegetables (CXC 44-1995).

1  For the purposes of this Standard, this includes recycled material of food-grade quality.

sections.title: CONTAMINANTS
sections.text: 7.1 The produce covered by this Standard shall comply with the maximum levels of the General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193-1995). 7.2 The produce covered by this Standard shall comply with the maximum residue limits for pesticides established by the Codex Alimentarius Commission.
sections.retrieval_text:
CONTAMINANTS
number=7
level=1
7.1 The produce covered by this Standard shall comply with the maximum levels of the General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193-1995). 7.2 The produce covered by this Standard shall comply with the maximum residue limits for pesticides established by the Codex Alimentarius Commission.

page_spans.text: 7.1 The produce covered by this Standard shall comply with the maximum levels of the General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193-1995). 7.2 The produce covered by this Standard shall comply with the maximum residue limits for pesticides established by the Codex Alimentarius Commission.

sections.title: HYGIENE
sections.text: 8.1 It is recommended that the produce covered by the provisions of this Standard be prepared and handled in accordance with the appropriate sections of the General Principles of Food Hygiene  (CXC 1-1969), Code of Hygienic Practice for Fresh Fruits and Vegetables  (CXC 53-2003), and other relevant Codex texts  such as Codes of Hygienic Practice and Codes of Practice. 8.2 The produce should comply with any microbiological criteria established in accordance with the Principles and Guidelines for the Establishment and Application of Microbiological Criteria Related to Foods (CXG 21-1997).

sentences.original: 8.1 It is recommended that the produce covered by the provisions of this Standard be prepared and handled in accordance with the appropriate sections of the General Principles of Food Hygiene  (CXC 1-1969), Code of Hygienic Practice for Fresh Fruits and Vegetables  (CXC 53-2003), and other relevant Codex texts  such as Codes of Hygienic Practice and Codes of Practice. 8.2

classification.sentence: The produce should comply with any microbiological criteria established in accordance with the Principles and Guidelines for the Establishment and Application of Microbiological Criteria Related to Foods (CXG 21-1997).
classification.pattern: Subject + modal verb (should) + verb (comply) + object (criteria) + relative clause specifying criteria source

components.subject: The produce
components.criteria_source: Principles and Guidelines for the Establishment and Application of Microbiological Criteria Related to Foods (CXG 21-1997)

classification.justification: The sentence imposes an obligation by requiring compliance with established microbiological criteria. Its structure features a subject followed by a modal verb and verb phrase describing compliance with specific criteria referenced by a guideline. No exact or close template from the catalog matches this specific compliance citation structure, resulting in a new pattern classification.
notes: The modal 'should' here conveys an obligation in normative regulatory context.
The sentence references an external standard for criteria but does not specify numerical limits, hence falls under obligation rather than limits and targets.

notes: The sentence functions as a normative extension clause referencing another standard and adding obligations, which is distinct from typical obligation templates focused on direct action requirements.

sentences.original: If the produce is not visible from the outside, each package shall be labelled as to the name of the produce and may be labelled as to name of the variety.

classification.modality: Condition for the first clause; Obligation and Permission for the second clause

components.Obligation: each package shall be labelled as to the name of the produce
components.Permission: each package may be labelled as to name of the variety

classification.justification: The sentence has a conditional antecedent followed by two coordinated modal expressions: obligation and permission. This combination with conditional modality and mixed normative statements does not strongly match known templates and requires a new structural pattern to capture the conditional plus dual modality coordination.
notes: The sentence blends condition, obligation, and permission within a single coordinated consequent, which is less common in typical regulatory templates and thus lacks close template match.

pages.title: STANDARD FOR GUAVAS
pages.summary: Página de título principal com documento e cabeçalho, sem seções ou texto interno estruturado para hierarquia.

A página contém duas seções principais: 'DEFINITION OF PRODUCE' e 'PROVISIONS CONCERNING QUALITY'. A seção 2 tem subseções numéricas detalhadas até o nível 3, com conteúdo claro associado a cada nó. Algumas seções principais e subseções continuam para a próxima página.

A página contém as seções 3, 4 e 5 do documento, com subseções claras em 4 e 5. A seção 5 continua para a próxima página. Texto está associado corretamente para cada nó. Sem sinal de continuidade vinda da página anterior nesta página.

A página contém continuação da subseção 5.2.1 da página anterior. Apresenta as seções 6, 7 e 8 com suas respectivas subseções claramente estruturadas. O texto está corretamente associado e segmentado em cada nó hierárquico. Não há indicação de continuidade para a próxima página nesta página.
